evals.expected_output: A well-structured review report scoring the component across 5 categories. accountCard is a clean component using @wire, SLDS, proper error/loading states — should score 16-20 range (Good). Report saved to code-review/ with correct filename format.
expectations: Report contains scores for all 5 categories: Bundle Structure, JS Code Quality, Template & Accessibility, Performance, Error Handling & UX
Each category score is between 1 and 5
Total score is the sum of all 5 category scores
Total score is between 16 and 22 (component is well-written)
Report mentions use of @wire as a positive pattern in Performance category
Report mentions loading and error states as positive in Error Handling category
Report is saved to a file matching pattern code-review/accountCard-YYYY-MM-DD-{score}.md
Report follows the template structure from assets/review-result-template.md
Naming conventions from references/lwc-naming-conventions.md are referenced in JS Code Quality
Report includes a Top Priority Actions section with at least 1 item

evals.expected_output: A critical review identifying multiple issues. dataExplorer has single-letter variables, no try/catch, no SLDS, inline styles, legacy if:true, no disconnectedCallback cleanup, god-component. Should score 6-12 range (Needs Work to Acceptable).
expectations: Report contains scores for all 5 categories
Total score is between 5 and 13 (component has significant issues)
JS Code Quality score is 1 or 2 — single-letter variable names (d, f, x, pg, srch) violate naming conventions
Report flags the use of abbreviated variable names as a naming convention violation
Performance score flags missing disconnectedCallback for the resize event listener
Error Handling score flags missing try/catch on async loadData method
Template & Accessibility score flags use of inline styles instead of SLDS
Template & Accessibility flags legacy if:true/if:false instead of lwc:if
Bundle Structure flags this as a god-component — everything in one file, 100+ lines
Report is saved to code-review/dataExplorer-YYYY-MM-DD-{score}.md

evals.expected_output: A review of a trivial component. Should handle the edge case where most categories are N/A or have nothing to check. Score around 13-17. The review should note simplicity without penalizing for features that aren't needed.
expectations: Report contains scores for all 5 categories
Total score is between 12 and 18
Performance category notes N/A or scores 3 with note about no async operations
Error Handling category notes N/A or scores 3 with note about no async operations
Report does NOT penalize the component for being simple
Naming conventions are checked even on this small component
Report is saved to code-review/simpleGreeting-YYYY-MM-DD-{score}.md
